title: Dialect Portability, NoSQL Tradeoffs, Distribution, and Production Defense · Data Contract Brief: Nosql Model Choice
summary: A team must move a browser prototype toward PostgreSQL, justify any non-relational component, and defend the complete data system. Planned artifact: Add and verify the sql nosql model choice increment in a production-ready cross-dialect data system portfolio. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose relational, document, key-value, graph, or mixed models from access, integrity, evolution, and consistency needs.
Separate standard relational intent from SQLite and PostgreSQL syntax, typing, NULL, date, and feature behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Declare result or mutation grain, keys, NULL meaning, and transaction boundary; predict cardinality and invariants; run the smallest query; reconcile counts and plans; then test changed data and dialect limits.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Move a SQLite 3.49.1 worked example artifact toward PostgreSQL 18.4 while preserving relational intent. Record one dialect difference, consistency boundary, restore signal, and changed case 2; justify any non-relational component. A defensible solution applies Nosql Model Choice, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a production-ready cross-dialect data system portfolio.

content.code:
SELECT ticket_id, status, opened_at
FROM tickets
WHERE status <> 'resolved'
ORDER BY opened_at, ticket_id;
-- Record SQLite behavior, then verify syntax and semantics against PostgreSQL 18.4.
content.caption: Runnable relational query or data-system contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

steps.title: Build Nosql Model Choice evidence · Theory Nosql Model Choice
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable SQLite increment headed "-- Evidence: sql-nosql-model-choice-aj058t" with a concrete grain, hypothesis, or prediction; dialect, storage-model, distribution, or production evidence; and an observation, reconciliation, or verification tied to returned rows, changed rows, an error, or a plan.

Evidence target: Build Nosql Model Choice evidence · Theory Nosql Model Choice.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Nosql Model Choice into observable performance evidence. For Build Nosql Model Choice evidence · Theory Nosql Model Choice, keep Nosql Model Choice connected to executable relational evidence.

content.code:
-- Evidence: sql-nosql-model-choice-aj058t
-- grain: declare the exact row or mutation grain before running
SELECT typeof(owner_id) AS sqlite_runtime_type, COUNT(*) AS rows
FROM tickets GROUP BY typeof(owner_id) ORDER BY sqlite_runtime_type;
-- observation: reconcile the returned rows, changed count, error, or plan with the prediction
